titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 616

p.seg: Alexander, by the Grace of God King of the Scots, To all worthy men of his whole land, Clerk and lay : Greeting.

Let present and future know that I have granted, and by this my charter made firm to Robert de Aubein, and to his heirs, those twenty merks which is owed annually as waiting which the Prior and Convent of Coldingham owe to me for their lands at Coldingham, until I or my heirs shall have given to Robert, or to his heirs, twenty merks’ worth of land on the south side of the Sea of Scotia, or between the Mounth and the Sea of Scotia. Wherefore I will, that the aforesaid Robert de Aubein, and his heirs, should receive those twenty merks each year at Easter. And I command the Prior of Coldingham, and his Baillies, that they should pay those twenty merks to them at Easter, without hindrance until I or my heirs shall have given to them the aforesaid twenty merks’ worth of land, just as the Charter of the lord King, William my father, produced on the matter for the aforesaid Robert, bears witness.
As witnesses : William del Bois, my Chancellor; William de Valognes, my Chamberlain; Philip de Mowbray; Geoffrey son of Richard, sheriff of Perth; Philip of Lundin.
At Perth, on the third day of March.
